common.data_description_host:
Enter your AdGuard Home address.

Examples:
- `{example_host}`
- `{example_host_port}`
- `{example_ip_port}`
- `{example_url}`

If you enter a hostname or IP address without a port, port `{default_port}` is used.
If you enter a URL starting with `https://` or `http://` without a port, the standard port for that scheme is used (`443` for HTTPS, `80` for HTTP).
If you do not enter a scheme, `http://` is assumed.
common.data_description_verify_ssl: Should SSL certificates be verified? This should be off for self-signed certificates.

hassio_confirm.description: Do you want to configure Home Assistant to connect to the AdGuard Home provided by the app: {addon}?
hassio_confirm.title: AdGuard Home via Home Assistant app

user.description: Set up your AdGuard Home instance.

name.description: The name of the filter subscription.
name.name: [%key:common::config_flow::data::name%]

url.description: The filter URL to subscribe to, containing the filter rules.
url.name: [%key:common::config_flow::data::url%]

add_url.name: Add URL

disable_url.description: Disables a filter subscription in AdGuard Home.

url.name: [%key:common::config_flow::data::url%]

disable_url.name: Disable URL

enable_url.description: Enables a filter subscription in AdGuard Home.

url.name: [%key:common::config_flow::data::url%]

enable_url.name: Enable URL

refresh.description: Refreshes all filter subscriptions in AdGuard Home.

force.description: Force update (bypasses AdGuard Home throttling), omit for a regular refresh.
force.name: Force

refresh.name: Refresh

remove_url.description: Removes a filter subscription from AdGuard Home.
